title: We’re winning!! The Cochise County (Arizona) Board Of Supervisors is calling a special meeting to discuss a hand count audit of the general election tabulations to compare against the machines and to withhold certification of the election until it is completed! — The Donald
text:
391
We’re winning!! The Cochise County (Arizona) Board Of Supervisors is calling a special meeting to discuss a hand count audit of the general election tabulations to compare against the machines and to withhold certification of the election until it is completed!
by
The county attorney is advising against this as “unlawful” but they are going to do it anyway and let the chips fall where they may. Fingers crossed. Unsure of the date of the meeting but I think it’s Tuesday.
Breaking News:
Just in: 2 out of 3 of the Cochise County Board of Supervisors has called for a Special Meeting
Agenda Item:
The Election Department shall take such action necessary to perform a 100% County -Wide hand count audit of the 2022 General Election to assure agreement with the voting machine count. Such audit will be completed prior to final certification of 2022 general election cavass by the board of Supervisors.
BahamaDonOP 6 points2 years ago
https://archive.li/o/6qYaB/https://destinyhosted.com/agenda_publish.cfm?id=26244&mt=BOS,BOE,FCD,LIB,LIGHT,PUBNTC,EXEC,SPCL,WKS&vl=true&get_month=10&get_year=2022&dsp=agm&seq=6721&rev=0&ag=2442&ln=79184&nseq=0&nrev=0&pseq=&prev=&vl=true%23ReturnTo79184
Link of minutes of the meeting where the two requested this to go forward.
ChynahIzAzzhole4 points2 years ago
Hand counting is unlawful.... yes, if you're a cocksucking commie, it sure is.
BahamaDonOP 4 points2 years ago
Gotcha a link showing proceedings where the two supervisors asked for this to proceed.
IncredibleMrE13 points2 years ago
Thanks. So it looks like they have requested a hand count audit of the results next month - has the vote to approve it occurred yet? When does the meeting take place?
BahamaDonOP 5 points2 years ago
3 board members. 2 are supposed to be voting for it. The other is closer to a commie. It just takes 2 to pass it. Unsure of the meeting schedule yet.
IncredibleMrE14 points2 years ago
So it will pass, just a matter of having the meeting and holding the vote. Got it. Please keep us posted!
War_Hamster4 points2 years ago
If Dr. Frank is correct, they won't find anything.
This is like counting the cash in your register over and over. It's always going to come up the same, even though some of the bills are counterfeit.
You have to find the counterfeit bills (votes), which means analyzing the voter rolls.
If they predetermine the vote count in the machine, they then produce the right amount of ballots to match that count, even though some of the alleged voters are counterfeit.
PorchLightOn3 points2 years ago
Yes, but I think we're dealing with a patchwork of different cheating methods in different locations. I believe they did predetermine the vote count in the machines in Maricopa, and then produce the right amount of ballots to match the count, just as you said.
But in Williamson County Tennessee, the EAC and Dominion were called in to verify a count problem in an Oct 2021 election, caught by poll workers. In 7 of 18 scanners, the machine total didn't match the number of ballots actually scanned by the machine. The error was able to be duplicated by the EAC and Dominion, with lots of vague lack of explanations for why this happened. Williamson has since kicked Dominion to the curb.
What's even bigger is a recent video by David Cross and Kevin Moncla in GA, saying that this same "Tennessee Problem" has now been found in 64 Georgia counties. I'm scrambling to keep up lately, and I'm embarrassed to say I haven't watched the video yet, but it's here, for anyone interested:
https://archive.li/o/6qYaB/https://rumble.com/v1mcmt6-a-shortened-presentation-of-explosive-new-evidence-found-on-dominion-machin.htmlThis is easier: Kanekoa The Great did a very helpful Substack article summing all this up, a few days ago:
https://archive.li/o/6qYaB/https://kanekoa.substack.com/p/bombshell-dominion-error-code-uncoveredSo... if this Dominion software problem is widespread, doing a simple handcount might catch other scanners coming up with a total that doesn't match the number of ballots fed into the scanner.
I'm more convinced than ever, the way they hide the cheat is to do different things in different counties.
War_Hamster2 points2 years ago
I could certainly see there being more than occasional errors trying to get the right amount of fake ballots inserted. There is a human element to this.
But again, if Dr. Frank is correct, the patterns are too uniform for there to be multiple algos running.
PorchLightOn3 points2 years ago
I think I need to review what Dr. Frank has been saying lately (I haven't seen any of his presentations for a long time, so I'm badly out of touch.)
I could certainly see there being more than occasional errors trying to get the right amount of fake ballots inserted. There is a human element to this.
(edited to clarify the following; my first attempt was terrible, lol.)
Dr. Frank's algorithms have to do with votes being manipulated for the candidates. This is a different issue; what they're calling "the Tennessee error" wasn't about an error in getting the right amount of fake ballots inserted, to match the vote counts for any particular candidate. This is only about the number of ballots counted.
This was a real-time software counting error that happened right in front of pollworkers, during the election. They happened to be writing down (on Post-It notes) the numbers of ballots scanned. Basically, the number that came out of the machine on the tape in the back didn't match the number of ballots inserted into the front of the machine. Seven of their 19 machines were undercounting the number of ballots.
But, you've given me a good idea to see if Frank has made any comments about this "Tennessee error," and the latest allegations that the Georgia Dominion software has the same "error." I'd like to know his thoughts.
So even a dumb hand count of ballots, done right after the election, might catch this "Tennessee error" problem. If some machines are only counting 90 ballots, after 100 were inserted, that could be caught right away. And that would be interesting...
(I hope that made sense...) ;-)
ShyRipley2 points2 years ago
Made sense to me. :)
This "Tennessee error" problem with the machines is so telling. If we cannot rely on the machines to accurately register the simple number of ballots/paper being fed into them, then how in the world can we rely on them to accurately count the VOTES on those pieces of paper???
Forget about organized fraud for a second... damn machines are a mess even if they are running 100% honestly.
Good on Cochise County for taking this step, hopefully. Every little win adds up.
War_Hamster1 points2 years ago
Yes, it made sense. I remember the Tennessee error.
I'm wondering if that one is more incompetence than malevolence, but we should have learned to suspect everything by now.
Errors that big should have removed them from consideration altogether. Most states had rules like error rats of less than 1/50,000 if I remember correctly, and this is WAY beyond that.
PorchLightOn2 points2 years ago
Thank you. I appreciate the pings, even when I manage to get here very very late!
MAGAvenger450 points2 years ago
They’ll now adjust for it and not get caught.
Ever heard of not broadcasting your moves to the enemy?
